--- title: "ex09. Unconditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend" --- > **Legacy vignette (for the website / historical notes).** > These files may not match the current exported API one-to-one. > Last verified: **2026-01-18**. > > For the up-to-date workflow, see the main package vignettes (basic, unconditional, conditional, and causal).. # Unconditional DPmixGPD: Stick-Breaking (SB) Backend with Tail Augmentation **Purpose**: Demonstrate the stick-breaking backend (`components = J`) while augmenting the extreme tail with a GPD. This mirrors the CRP+GPD pipeline (ex06) but highlights the fixed-truncation behavior for bulk components. --- ## Data Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Tail-heavy data data("nc_pos_tail200_k4", package = "DPmixGPD") y_tail <- nc_pos_tail200_k4$y summary_tbl <- tibble( statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"), value = c(length(y_tail), mean(y_tail), sd(y_tail), min(y_tail), max(y_tail)) ) df_data <- data.frame(y = y_tail) p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) + geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") + geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) + labs(title = "Tail-Designed Data", x = "y", y = "Density") + theme_minimal() gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_raw, ncol = 1) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/data-setup-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Tail Dataset Summaries
statistic value
N 200.000
Mean 2.334
SD 2.300
Min 0.328
Max 19.870
````` ::: ::: --- ## Threshold Selection ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} thresholds <- quantile(y_tail, c(0.70, 0.75, 0.80, 0.85)) u_threshold <- thresholds["80%"] ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) + geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") + geom_vline(xintercept = u_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + labs(title = paste("Threshold at", round(u_threshold, 2)), x = "y", y = "Density") + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/threshold-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Model Specification & Bundle This follows the same structure as the SB bulk-only vignette (`ex05`): build a bundle with `DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle()`, run MCMC, then use the S3 `predict()` + `plot()` helpers. Compared with `ex06` (CRP+GPD), here we keep the **stick-breaking** backend and use a **Gamma** bulk kernel with a lognormal threshold prior, then contrast it with a bulk-only **Laplace** fit. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} bundle_sb_gpd <- DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle( y = y_tail, kernel = "gamma", backend = "sb", GPD = TRUE, components = 5, param_specs = list( gpd = list( threshold = list( mode = "dist", dist = "lognormal", args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25) ) ) ), mcmc = mcmc ) ``` ::: --- ## Running MCMC ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fit_sb_gpd <- load_or_fit("ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB-fit_sb_gpd", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb_gpd)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, scale, shape, tail_scale, tail_shape, threshold, w, z ===== Samplers ===== RW sampler (18) - alpha - shape[] (5 elements) - scale[] (5 elements) - threshold - tail_scale - tail_shape - v[] (4 elements) categorical sampler (200) - z[] (200 elements) ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} summary(fit_sb_gpd) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MixGPD summary | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Gamma Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE | epsilon: 0.025 n = 200 | components = 5 Summary Initial components: 5 | Components after truncation: 4 WAIC: 663.354 lppd: -312.156 | pWAIC: 19.521 Summary table
parameter mean sd q0.025 q0.500 q0.975 ess
weights[1] 0.391 0.078 0.28 0.375 0.538 2.359
weights[2] 0.269 0.039 0.205 0.265 0.353 9.042
weights[3] 0.18 0.052 0.087 0.19 0.26 2.671
weights[4] 0.129 0.052 0.054 0.12 0.226 6.368
alpha 1.904 0.812 0.547 1.864 3.536 50.116
tail_scale 1.423 0.163 1.123 1.408 1.728 28.375
tail_shape 0.23 0.09 0.055 0.212 0.375 9.136
threshold 0.746 0.033 0.691 0.736 0.807 1.926
shape[1] 2.889 1.072 1.737 2.566 4.782 24.412
shape[2] 3.237 1.196 1.542 3.177 5.834 13.145
shape[3] 3.205 1.131 1.605 2.958 6.149 55.82
shape[4] 2.952 0.958 1.34 2.964 4.629 27.463
scale[1] 1.401 0.901 0.279 1.148 3.439 11.876
scale[2] 1.648 1.258 0.253 1.474 4.441 39.134
scale[3] 1.367 1.236 0.253 0.975 4.79 54.823
scale[4] 1.42 1.054 0.239 1.166 3.851 34.189
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} params_sb_gpd <- params(fit_sb_gpd) params_sb_gpd ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Posterior mean parameters $alpha [1] "1.904" $w [1] "0.391" "0.269" "0.18" "0.129" $shape [1] "2.889" "3.237" "3.205" "2.952" $scale [1] "1.401" "1.648" "1.367" "1.42" $tail_scale [1] "1.423" $tail_shape [1] "0.23" ``` ::: ::: --- ## Posterior Predictions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} y_grid <- seq(0, max(y_tail) * 1.3, length.out = 300) pred_density <- predict(fit_sb_gpd, y = y_grid, type = "density") safe_plot(pred_density) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/density-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} y_surv <- seq(u_threshold, max(y_tail) * 1.1, length.out = 60) pred_surv <- predict(fit_sb_gpd, y = y_surv, type = "survival") safe_plot(pred_surv) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/survival-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} quant_probs <- c(0.90, 0.95, 0.99) pred_quant <- predict(fit_sb_gpd, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") safe_plot(pred_quant) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/quantile-predict-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Tail vs Bulk Comparison ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} bundle_sb_bulk <- DPmixGPD::build_nimble_bundle( y = y_tail, kernel = "laplace", backend = "sb", GPD = FALSE, components = 5, mcmc = mcmc ) fit_sb_bulk <- load_or_fit("ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB-fit_sb_bulk", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb_bulk)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ===== Monitors ===== thin = 1: alpha, location, scale, w, z ===== Samplers ===== RW sampler (10) - alpha - location[] (5 elements) - v[] (4 elements) conjugate sampler (5) - scale[] (5 elements) categorical sampler (200) - z[] (200 elements) ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} bulk_quant <- predict(fit_sb_bulk, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) t_quant <- predict(fit_sb_gpd, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) bind_rows( bulk_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk-only"), t_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk + GPD") ) %>% select(any_of(c("model", "index", "estimate", "lwr", "upr", "lower", "upper"))) %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>% kable(caption = "Quantiles: Bulk-only vs GPD-augmented", align = "c") %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Quantiles: Bulk-only vs GPD-augmented
model index estimate lower upper
Bulk-only 0.90 6.82 5.39 8.68
Bulk-only 0.95 8.71 7.00 10.33
Bulk-only 0.99 14.11 11.11 17.97
Bulk + GPD 0.90 4.79 4.15 5.49
Bulk + GPD 0.95 6.58 5.53 7.69
Bulk + GPD 0.99 12.14 8.92 15.90
````` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(bulk_quant) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/comparison-plots-1.png){width=768} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(t_quant) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/comparison-plots-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Diagnostics ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} safe_plot(fit_sb_gpd, family = c("histogram", "autocorrelation", "running")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/diagnostics-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/diagnostics-2.png){width=768} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex09-unconditional-DPmixGPD-SB_files/figure-html/diagnostics-3.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Key Takeaways - Stick-breaking truncation fixes the number of bulk components but still flexibly models the tail via GPD. - Posterior `predict()` + `plot()` workflows visualize densities, survival probabilities, and posterior-mean extreme quantiles. - Comparing bulk-only vs GPD-augmented quantiles reveals how tail augmentation shifts the 95-99% levels. - Next: conditional DPmix (ex08-ex11) to explore covariate effects before moving to causal regimes. --- ## Workflow Navigation - Previous: [ex08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP](ex08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP.html) - Next: [ex10-conditional-DPmix-CRP](ex10-conditional-DPmix-CRP.html) - Workflow index: [Roadmap](../roadmap.html) - Practical entry: [Cookbook](../cookbook.html)
